title: Review a migration for lock safety
description: Read a migration and report which locks it takes and whether it is safe to run on a live, populated table.
body: Use when validating a schema change before deploy. Actions: inspect each DDL statement and determine its lock level — flag plain CREATE INDEX (use CONCURRENTLY), ALTER TABLE forms that rewrite or validate while holding ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, and adding NOT NULL/CHECK without the NOT VALID then VALIDATE split; confirm lock_timeout/statement_timeout are set. Do not assume a migration that passes on an empty dev table is safe on a large hot one. Report and record each statement's lock level and a safer rewrite. This skill is read-only and does not run the migration. Validation signals include each step taking a short, weak lock. Risk boundary: any unavoidable long ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on a hot table is a blocking finding requiring a maintenance window and human gate.
